channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Introducing SQL MCP Server
item.description: SQL MCP Server gives enterprises a secure, feature-rich way to enable agents to access data. This is accomplished without exposing the schema, risking consistency, or relying on fragile natural language parsing. SQL MCP Server is a feature of Data API builder, so deployments have a proven entity...
item.dc:creator: Jerry Nixon

item.title: Give your Foundry Agent Custom Tools with MCP Servers on Azure Functions
item.description: Learn how to connect your MCP server hosted on Azure Functions to Microsoft Foundry agents. This post covers authentication options and setup steps to give your AI agent access to custom MCP tools. The post Give your Foundry Agent Custom Tools with MCP Servers on Azure Functions appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Lily Ma

item.title: Azure Container Storage v2.1.0: Now GA with Elastic SAN
item.description: Explore what’s new in Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 with Elastic SAN integration, modular on-demand installation, and enhanced node selector support.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Turn your agents into AKS experts: Agent Skills for AKS
item.description: Announcing agent skills for AKS, easily pluggable into any agent to enhance them with built-in AKS expertise, safer workflows, and reduced developer overhead.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: MCP as Easy as 1-2-3: Introducing the Fluent API for MCP Apps
item.description: Build MCP tools with rich UI experiences using the new Fluent API for MCP Apps in the .NET isolated worker. Configure views, permissions, and security policies with just a few lines of code. The post MCP as Easy as 1-2-3: Introducing the Fluent API for MCP Apps appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lilian Kasem (she/her)

item.title: Agentic development aspirations: build, run, observe — without more Markdown
item.description: AI agents can write code but struggle to run and test it. Aspire gives your agent a codified app model to build, run, and observe your whole stack. The post Agentic development aspirations: build, run, observe — without more Markdown appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Montaquila

item.title: Scalable AI with Azure Cosmos DB: Tredence Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) | March 2026
item.description: Azure Cosmos DB enables scalable AI-driven document processing, addressing one of the biggest barriers to operational scale in today’s enterprise AI landscape. Organizations continue to manage massive volumes of structured and unstructured content—contracts, regulatory filings, operational records,...
item.dc:creator: Manish Sharma

item.title: Take full control of your floating windows in Visual Studio
item.description: Make Visual Studio floating windows work perfectly with PowerToys FancyZones. Flip one option to get independent windows, better snapping, and less friction. The post Take full control of your floating windows in Visual Studio appeared first on Visual Studio Blog.
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Predictive AI using Triton and ResNet-50
item.description: Run predictive AI at the edge by deploying Triton with the ONNX backend on AKS enabled by Azure Arc for local ResNet 50 GPU inference without cloud dependency.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Generative AI with Open‑Source LLM Server
item.description: Run generative AI at the edge with Ollama and vLLM on AKS enabled by Azure Arc, enabling local LLM inference on GPU hardware without dependency or data egress.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Series Introduction and Scope
item.description: Scenario driven series for generative and predictive AI inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc, across CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs in on-premises and edge environments.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Bringing AI to the Edge and On‑Premises
item.description: Learn how to bring AI inference closer to your data with AKS enabled by Azure Arc, with practical hybrid and edge scenarios for secure, low-latency workloads.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript
item.description: After July 9, 2026, the Azure SDK for JavaScript will no longer support Node.js 20.x. Upgrade to an Active Node.js Long Term Support (LTS) version to stay secure and up-to-date. The post Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Minh-Anh Phan

item.title: MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quickstart with TypeScript
item.description: Learn how to build and deploy MCP (Model Context Protocol) apps on Azure Functions using TypeScript. This guide covers MCP tools, resources, local development, and serverless deployment with a practical weather app example. The post MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quickstart with TypeScript appeared...
item.dc:creator: Swapnil Nagar

item.title: Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain)
item.description: Aspire 13.2 ships aspire docs, a CLI for browsing, searching, and reading official aspire.dev docs from your terminal, with the same source of truth available to AI skills and automation. The post Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain) appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Pine

item.title: Improving the Markdown Editor for Work Items
item.description: We introduced the Markdown editor in July 2025 to bring Markdown support to large text fields in work items. Since then, we’ve received valuable customer feedback highlighting challenges with the editing experience, particularly when switching in and out of edit mode. Many users found the current...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: The Polyglot tax – Part 3
item.description: Vectors, Analytics, and the End of ETL Part 3 of 4 – The Multimodal Database Series In Part 1: The Polyglot Tax we laid out the fraud detection scenario: a transaction comes in, and before you approve or deny it you need five checks. Order history (relational). Device fingerprint (JSON)....
item.dc:creator: Aditya Badramraju

item.title: Bookmark Studio: evolving bookmarks in Visual Studio
item.description: Bookmarks in Visual Studio have always been a simple, reliable feature. Many developers use them regularly, and over the years we’ve heard consistent feedback from those users. Bookmarks were useful, but there were a few core gaps that kept them from being as effective and relevant as they could...
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen

item.title: Project Nighthawk: A Research Agent Built for Field Engineering
item.description: If you work in field engineering, you know the scenario. A customer is deploying AKS in a regulated environment. They hit an issue during node bootstrapping. They want to know exactly what happens when a node joins the cluster, which components run in which order, and whether the behaviour they’re...
item.dc:creator: Diego Casati, Ray Kao

item.title: Building with Azure Skills
item.description: Part 3 of the Azure Skills Plugin series Previously: How to Install the Azure Plugin You’ve installed the Azure Skills Plugin. The Azure MCP server is running. You have a huge collection of tools and skills at your disposal. So, now what do you actually say to it? This post is a prompt cookbook....
item.dc:creator: Chris Harris

item.title: Aspire 13.2: Dashboard Gets Smarter Export and Telemetry
item.description: Aspire 13.2 brings powerful new dashboard features for exporting and importing telemetry, querying telemetry via API, and improved GenAI telemetry visualization. The post Aspire 13.2: Dashboard Gets Smarter Export and Telemetry appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: James Newton-King

item.title: Visual Studio March Update – Build Your Own Custom Agents
item.description: This month’s Visual Studio update gives you new ways to customize GitHub Copilot. Custom agents allow you to build specialized Copilot agents tailored to your team’s workflow, backed by the tools and knowledge sources that matter to your project. Alongside that, agent skills bring reusable...
item.dc:creator: Mark Downie

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd) – March 2026: Run and Debug AI Agents Locally, GitHub Copilot Integration, & Container App Jobs
item.description: Run, invoke, and monitor AI agents locally or in Microsoft Foundry with the new azd AI agent extension commands. Plus GitHub Copilot-powered project setup, Container App Jobs deployment, local preflight validation, and configurable timeouts. The post Azure Developer CLI (azd) – March 2026: Run and...
item.dc:creator: PuiChee (PC) Chan

item.title: Writing Azure service-related unit tests with Docker using Spring Cloud Azure
item.description: This post shows how to write Azure service-related unit tests with Docker using Spring Cloud Azure. The post Writing Azure service-related unit tests with Docker using Spring Cloud Azure appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rujun Chen

item.title: Control where your pods land on AKS with NAP
item.description: Combine taints, affinity, and topology spread constraints with AKS Node Auto-Provisioning to control where your pods land.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure SDK Release (March 2026)
item.description: Azure SDK releases every month. In this post, you find this month's highlights and release notes. The post Azure SDK Release (March 2026) appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Ronnie Geraghty

item.title: TypeScript AppHost in Aspire 13.2: same app model, different syntax
item.description: Aspire 13.2 enables TypeScript developers to define AppHosts directly in TypeScript, with the same streamlined Aspire application model. The post TypeScript AppHost in Aspire 13.2: same app model, different syntax appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Sebastien Ros

item.title: Building Search-Enabled Agents with Azure AI Foundry and Semantic Kernel and A2A
item.description: A step-by-step guide to enable search in the semantic kernel agents using bing grounding in Azure Foundry AI Agent using A2A. The post Building Search-Enabled Agents with Azure AI Foundry and Semantic Kernel and A2A appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Munish Malhotra

item.title: Your Entire Engineering Floor Just Stopped Coding
item.description: And the developers running Claude Code and GitHub Copilot CLI didn’t notice… Status Page Says ‘Operational.’ Your Subagents Say Otherwise. If you’re running autonomous agents in any serious capacity, you’ve experienced this: model provider outages aren’t edge cases — they’re part of the operating...
item.dc:creator: Desi Villanueva

item.title: Unlock More Power in Your Development Workflow: Syncfusion for Visual Studio Subscribers
item.description: A few months ago, I was talking with a developer who said something that stuck with me: “I love building apps. I just don’t love rebuilding the same UI controls over and over again.” That’s the reality for a lot of teams. You want to focus on your business logic, your architecture, your...
item.dc:creator: Jim Harrer

item.title: SQL code analysis in VS Code: Configure rules without editing your project file
item.description: SQL code analysis has been part of the SSDT workflow for a long time. Before deploying a schema change, you could run a set of static analysis rules against your project to catch potential issues, things like missing primary keys, deprecated syntax, or objects that could break under certain...
item.dc:creator: Iqra Shaikh

item.title: Manage SQL database schemas in VS Code: Publish dialog and item templates
item.description: Making schema changes often means jumping between tools. You write code in VS Code, then switch to a separate tool to deploy your changes : exporting a script, running it manually, or copy-pasting into a query editor. Either way, it pulls you out of your flow. With the latest updates to SQL...
item.dc:creator: Iqra Shaikh

item.title: Agentic Platform Engineering with GitHub Copilot
item.description: We’ve talked about the human scale problem and what happens when infrastructure scales but understanding doesn’t. If you’ve been following along, you know the thesis: our tools have outpaced our ability to operate them, and platform engineering is how we’re fighting back. But here’s the thing –...
item.dc:creator: Diego Casati, Ray Kao

item.title: From code to cloud: Deploy an AI agent to Microsoft Foundry in minutes with azd
item.description: Deploy and monitor an AI agent on Microsoft Foundry using the Azure Developer CLI (azd). This tutorial covers infrastructure scaffolding with Bicep, one-command deployment, local development, and real-time log streaming—all from Visual Studio Code. The post From code to cloud: Deploy an AI agent to...
item.dc:creator: PuiChee (PC) Chan

item.title: Announcing Aspire 13.2
item.description: Aspire 13.2 brings TypeScript AppHost authoring, an agent-native CLI, Foundry integration, and more. The post Announcing Aspire 13.2 appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Montaquila, David Fowler

item.title: Remote MCP Server preview in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Earlier this week we release the public preview for our Azure DevOps MCP Server. Today we are excited to let you know that the Azure DevOps MCP Server is now available to use in Microsoft Foundry. For those who are new to Foundry, Microsoft Foundry is a unified platform for building and managing AI...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: From Azure IoT Operations Data Processor Pipelines to Dataflows
item.description: In this post we explore the evolution from Azure IoT Operations Data Processor Pipelines to Dataflows, why we adopted a hybrid strategy with custom Rust pods, and the architectural lessons we learned building event detection systems at the edge. The post From Azure IoT Operations Data Processor...
item.dc:creator: Maho Pacheco

item.title: Authentication Tokens Are Not a Data Contract
item.description: Authentication tokens exist to answer one question: is this caller authorized to do this? They are not intended to be a stable data interface, a schema you can depend on, or an input into application logic. If your application decodes tokens and reads claims from them, this is an important...
item.dc:creator: Angel Wong

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd): Run and test AI agents locally with azd
item.description: New azd ai agent run and invoke commands let you start and test AI agents from your terminal—locally or in the cloud. The post Azure Developer CLI (azd): Run and test AI agents locally with azd appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: PuiChee (PC) Chan, Travis Angevine

item.title: DiskANN Vector Index Improvements
item.description: Remember when we announced the Public Preview of DiskANN vector indexes back in November and mentioned that once you created the index, your table became read‑only? Yeah… about that… 😅 We shipped early because the demand for Vector search in SQL was overwhelming. We knew the constraints weren’t...
item.dc:creator: Davide Mauri, Pooja Kamath

item.title: When Infrastructure Scales But Understanding Doesn’t
item.description: We all know this, even if we don’t like to admit it: modern infrastructure can scale infinitely, but human understanding doesn’t. We’ve all seen it happen – organizations going from managing dozens of servers to thousands of containers, from deploying weekly to deploying hundreds of times per day,...
item.dc:creator: Diego Casati, Ray Kao

item.title: MSSQL Extension for VS Code: SQL Notebooks, AI-Powered Schema Design, Data API builder & More
item.description: The MSSQL extension for VS Code v1.41 continues to evolve, delivering features that make SQL development more integrated, more powerful, and more developer-friendly. In this release, we’re introducing the Public Preview of Schema Designer with GitHub Copilot, Data API builder, and SQL Notebooks,...
item.dc:creator: Yo-Lei Chen, Tauseef Siddique, Carlos Robles

item.title: Announcing Gateway API support for App Routing (preview)
item.description: The AKS app routing add-on now supports the Kubernetes Gateway API via a meshless Istio control plane — the recommended path to migrate from Ingress-NGINX.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: From 150 Unread to Zero Stress: Automating Inbox Triage with MCP and GitHub Copilot
item.description: Taming the Noisy Inbox: How I Used MCP to Automate Email and Teams Triage How the Model Context Protocol (MCP) turns your AI coding assistant into a workplace productivity engine — connecting Microsoft 365 data to your terminal workflow. The Problem We All Share If you work in a customer-facing...
item.dc:creator: Desi Villanueva

item.title: Azure Skills Plugin – Let’s Get Started!
item.description: Part 2 of the Azure Skills Plugin series Previously: Announcing the Azure Skills Plugin This post is all about getting you up and running. I won’t go deep on capabilities, architecture or anything like that here – that’s coming in future posts in this series. The goal is to get the plugin...
item.dc:creator: Chris Harris

item.title: PowerToys 0.98 is here: new Keyboard Manager UX, the Command Palette Dock and better CursorWrap!
item.description: We’re back with a fresh PowerToys release – PowerToys 0.98! This month introduces the new Command Palette Dock, along with a refreshed Keyboard Manager experience and many other improvements across the utilities. You can grab the update by checking for updates in PowerToys or by heading to the...
item.dc:creator: Niels Laute

item.title: Introducing the Azure Cosmos DB Plugin for Cursor
item.description: We’re excited to announce the Cursor plugin for Azure Cosmos DB bringing AI-powered database expertise, best practices guidance, and live database connectivity directly into your coding workflow. Whether you’re designing a new data model, optimizing queries, or building a RAG application with...
item.dc:creator: Sajeetharan Sinnathurai

item.title: Azure DevOps Remote MCP Server (public preview)
item.description: When we released the local Azure DevOps MCP Server, it gave customers a way to connect Azure DevOps data with tools like Visual Studio and Visual Studio Code through GitHub Copilot Chat. The next step was to make this experience easier to get started with and to enable it for services that support...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: Foundry Agent Service is GA: private networking, Voice Live, and enterprise-grade evaluations
item.description: The next-gen Foundry Agent Service is generally available today with end-to-end private networking, Voice Live integration, expanded MCP authentication, GA evaluations with continuous monitoring, and hosted agent deployments in six new Azure regions. The post Foundry Agent Service is GA: private...
item.dc:creator: Bala Venkataraman, jeffhollan, Nick Brady

item.title: Awesome GitHub Copilot just got a website, and a learning hub, and plugins!
item.description: Back in July, we launched the Awesome GitHub Copilot Customizations repo with a simple goal: give the community a place to share custom instructions, prompts, and chat modes to customize the AI responses from GitHub Copilot. We were hoping for maybe one community contribution per week. That… did...
item.dc:creator: Matt Soucoup
